Clinical Supervision Laboratory: Innovative Unit in Psychotherapy and Counseling

The Clinical Supervision Laboratory at our university is a comprehensive unit that offers in-depth training and development opportunities to psychology students and professionals. This laboratory provides the opportunity to observe, practice and supervise psychotherapy processes in real time. These opportunities for clinical practice are especially critical for students in master's and doctoral programs.

The

mirrored rooms

in our laboratory provide an ideal environment for students to receive real-time feedback during therapeutic interviews. Observing the relationship with the client in the best possible way contributes to the development of clinical skills. Furthermore,

the interview rooms

simulate real-life therapy settings, giving students the opportunity to practice.

Through

testing applications

and

filing systems

, students gain experience in how to administer psychological assessments and analyze the results. Our lab also has a system that allows students to video record the counseling process and examine the sessions in detail. These video recordings are an important feedback tool for both students and supervisors.

Students have the opportunity

to consult with senior therapists

during therapeutic interviews. This allows them to gain a deeper understanding, combining theoretical knowledge with practical application.

The Clinical Supervision Lab is a space that is actively used by students of the

Family Counseling Master's Program

,

Clinical Psychology Master's Program

,

Addiction Counseling Master's Program

and

. This unit not only provides students with clinical skills, but also offers an environment that supports their professional development and reinforces their academic success.

The Clinical Supervision Laboratory is a unique opportunity for psychology students who want to lay a solid foundation for their academic and professional lives. You can take advantage of the opportunities offered in this unit to improve your skills in the fields of psychotherapy, counseling and psychological assessment.
